title: Number of farmed crustaceans killed for food
subtitle: The data includes farmed crustaceans such as shrimps, crabs, lobsters, prawns, and crayfish.
caption:
Number of farmed crustaceans killed for food, 2022
The data includes farmed crustaceans such as shrimps, crabs, lobsters, prawns, and crayfish.

This bar chart presents estimates for the number of farmed crustaceans killed for food in 2022, broken down by the World total and three specific countries: China, Indonesia, and Ecuador. Due to large uncertainties in the data, the chart displays upper and lower bounds alongside a mid-point estimate for each entity, with the mid-point values representing the average of those bounds. The vertical axis lists the geographic entities, while the horizontal bars extend to the right to show the count in billions. For the World total, the mid-point estimate stands at 630 billion, situated between a lower bound of 310 billion and an upper bound of 950 billion. China shows the highest figures among the individual countries listed, with a mid-point estimate of 320 billion, ranging from a lower bound of 190 billion to an upper bound of 450 billion. Ecuador follows with a mid-point estimate of 62 billion, bounded by 22 billion on the low end and 100 billion on the high end. Indonesia records a mid-point estimate of 49 billion, with a lower bound of 20 billion and an upper bound of 77 billion. The visual arrangement places the World total at the top left, followed by China on the top right, Indonesia on the bottom left, and Ecuador on the bottom right. The bars use a color scheme where the mid-point estimates are highlighted in red, while the upper and lower bounds are shown in grey. The data shows that the mid-point estimate for China is roughly half of the global mid-point estimate, while the figures for Indonesia and Ecuador are significantly smaller in magnitude. The gap between the upper and lower bounds is wide for all categories, illustrating the range of uncertainty in the counts. The largest visible value on the chart is the upper bound for the World total at 950 billion.
